data.title: ADM is streamlining its soy protein production network to enhance efficiency and serve customers better. They will leverage their Decatur facility and other global sites.
data.description: ADM (NYSE: ADM) revealed plans to optimize its global soy protein production network to boost efficiency and cater better to customers globally. The company aims to leverage the operational excellence of its Decatur, Illinois plant alongside other facilities worldwide. This initiative is part of ADM's strategy to enhance service quality for customers while maximizing operational capabilities.

data.title: Ethereum's price drops below $4,400 as 1M ETH to be withdrawn from staking amid rising publicly traded treasuries.
data.description: The cryptocurrency Ethereum has experienced a more significant decline compared to other digital currencies in the market. This drop coincides with a significant event where approximately 1 million ETH is scheduled to be removed from staking.
Max Miller, a member of Congress, has recently revealed undisclosed stock transactions.

data.title: Leggett & Platt sells Aerospace Products Group, updates 2025 guidance. Transaction completed with Tinicum Incorporated.
data.description: Leggett & Platt, based in Carthage, Missouri, revealed on August 29, 2025, that it has finalized the sale of its Aerospace Products Group to funds managed by Tinicum Incorporated. This successful transaction will likely impact the company's full year 2025 guidance. The details regarding the financial aspects and effects of the sale were not disclosed.

data.title: EVERCOAT strengthens U.S. sales team, aimed at body shops in the collision repair industry. VP states understanding of industry challenges. CINCINNATI.
data.description: A major player in automotive refinishing, EVERCOAT® has unveiled a reorganization of its U.S. Sales Team to enhance its presence in the collision repair industry. The company aims to better connect with body shops and cater to their needs amidst challenges such as tight deadlines and labor shortages. Dan Clarke, the Vice President and General Manager at EVERCOAT®, emphasized the importance of understanding the pressures faced by collision centers, including the drive for increased efficiency. This strategic move is geared towards strengthening the brand's position in the market.

data.title: Tyson Ciepluch promoted to President & Chief Operations Officer at Wantable after serving as COO since 2019.
data.description: Milwaukee-based personal styling service Wantable has promoted Tyson Ciepluch to President & Chief Operations Officer. Since joining in 2017, Ciepluch has been COO, overseeing significant growth. In his role, he led departments such as Fulfillment, Styling, and Customer Service.

data.title: Iran weakened after Hamas attack on Israel on Oct. 7, 2023. Stocks hit despite Iran's situation.
data.description: Following Hamas's attack on Israel on October 7, 2023, Iran's strategic standing has significantly deteriorated. The impact of the assault was profound, leading to a notable decline in Iran's influence and power on the global stage. This event has sent shockwaves through the region, reshaping the geopolitical landscape. Additionally, the attack prompted a notable downturn in the stock market, with various industries experiencing losses as Israel came under fire. The aftermath of the incident has left many reassessing the stability and security of the region and has raised concerns about escalating tensions.

data.title: Herc Holdings to participate in Morgan Stanley 13th Annual Laguna Conference with key executives attending.
data.description: In Bonita Springs, Florida, Herc Holdings Inc. (NYSE: HRI), a prominent player in the equipment rental sector, revealed that their president and CEO Larry Silber, along with senior VP and COO Aaron Birnbaum, and senior VP and CFO Mark Humphrey, will take part in the Morgan Stanley 13th Annual Laguna Conference in Laguna Niguel, CA. The event will feature a fireside chat discussion and a simultaneous webcast, showcasing company leadership to investors and industry stakeholders.

data.title: Academy Sports and Outdoors likely to report higher Q2 earnings with $2.14 per share, expecting $1.61 billion revenue.
data.description: Academy Sports and Outdoors, Inc. (NASDAQ: ASO) is set to announce its second-quarter earnings before the market opens on Tuesday, September 2. Analysts are anticipating earnings of $2.14 per share, marking an increase from $2.03 per share in the same period last year. The company, headquartered in Katy, Texas, is expected to reveal quarterly revenue of $1.61 billion, up from $1.55 billion in the previous year. Moreover, Academy Sports + Outdoors recently appointed Brandy Treadway as the executive vice president, chief legal officer, and corporate secretary. Following this news, the company's shares dipped by 4.7% to close at $53.66 on Thursday. For more details and updates, interested individuals can visit the Analyst Stock Ratings page on Benzinga's platform.
